audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for vineyard tenants, cornerstone, Caesar/image, resurrection dispute, greatest command, David/Lord question, scribes warning, and widow offering.

verses.restored: He began to speak to them in parables: A person planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a winepress, built a tower, leased it to farmers, and went away.
At the season he sent a servant to the farmers so that he might receive from the farmers some of the fruit of the vineyard.
They took him, beat him, and sent him away empty.
Again he sent to them another servant; that one they struck on the head and dishonored.
He sent another, and that one they killed; and many others, some they beat and some they killed.
He still had one, a beloved son. Last he sent him to them, saying, They will respect my son.
But those farmers said to themselves, This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
Taking him, they killed him and threw him outside the vineyard.
What will the lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the farmers and give the vineyard to others.
Have you not read this Scripture: The stone the builders rejected, this became the head of the corner;
from the Lord this came to be, and it is marvelous in our eyes?
They were seeking to seize him, yet feared the crowd, for they knew he spoke the parable against them. Leaving him, they went away.
They send to him some of the Pharisees and Herodians, so that they might catch him in word.

Coming, they said to him, Teacher, we know you are true and do not care about anyone's status, for you do not look at people's face, but teach the way of the Divine in truth. Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar, or not? Should we give, or should we not give?
Knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, Why do you test me? Bring me a denarius so I may see it.
They brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? They said to him, Caesar's.
Jesus said to them, Give back the things of Caesar to Caesar, and the things of the Divine to the Divine. And they marveled at him.
Sadducees came to him, those saying there is no resurrection, and they questioned him, saying,
Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if someone's brother dies and leaves a wife but no child, his brother should take the wife and raise up seed for his brother.
There were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and dying, left no seed.
The second took her and died, leaving no seed; and the third likewise.
The seven left no seed. Last of all, the woman also died.
In the resurrection, when they rise, of which of them will she be wife? For the seven had her as wife.
Jesus said to them, Is this not why you wander: because you know neither the Scriptures nor the power of the Divine?
For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like messengers in the heavens.

Concerning the dead, that they are raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the bush passage, how the Divine spoke to him, saying, I am the Divine of Abraham, the Divine of Isaac, and the Divine of Jacob?
He is not Divine of dead ones, but of living ones. You wander greatly.
One of the scribes came, hearing them disputing, and seeing that he answered them beautifully, asked him, Which command is first of all?
Jesus answered, First is: Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.
You shall love the Lord your God from all your heart, from all your soul/life, from all your mind, and from all your strength.
Second is this: You shall love your neighbor as yourself. No other command is greater than these.
The scribe said to him, Beautifully said, Teacher; according to truth you said that he is one, and there is no other besides him.
And to love him from the whole heart, whole understanding, and whole strength, and to love the neighbor as oneself, is more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.
Jesus, seeing that he answered thoughtfully, said to him, You are not far from the reign of the Divine. And no one dared to question him anymore.
Answering as he taught in the temple, Jesus said, How do the scribes say that the Messiah is son of David?
David himself said in the holy Spirit: The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet.
David himself calls him Lord; so how is he his son? The great crowd heard him gladly.
In his teaching he said, Beware of the scribes, who desire to walk around in long robes, greetings in the marketplaces,
first seats in the synagogues and first places at feasts,
who devour widows' houses and for appearance make long prayers. These will receive greater judgment.
Sitting opposite the treasury, he watched how the crowd cast copper into the treasury. Many rich people cast in much.
One poor widow came and cast in two small coins, which are a quadrans.
Calling his disciples, he said to them, Amen, I say to you: this poor widow cast in more than all those casting into the treasury.
For all cast in out of their abundance; but she, out of her lack, cast in all she had, her whole life/living.
